title: split_addresses: allocates Vec<String> on every address test eval
description: eval_address_test calls message::split_addresses(hdr_value) for each matching header, which allocates a Vec<String> of owned Strings (one per address). For single-address headers (the common case), this is one unnecessary String allocation. At evaluation time there is no regex cache or pre-parsing for addresses. A lazy iterator that yields &str slices (or Cow<str>) would avoid the allocation in the common case. Impact: measurable in tight loops filtering large volumes of messages.
